title: Own every resource with RAII and smart pointers
description: Tie each heap allocation, file, lock, or handle to an object's lifetime via unique_ptr/shared_ptr and scope guards instead of manual new/delete.
body: Use when allocating or managing any resource in C++. Do not pair raw new/delete by hand, return owning raw pointers, or use shared_ptr where unique_ptr suffices (atomic refcount overhead). Actions: prefer make_unique/make_shared, express single ownership with unique_ptr and shared ownership only when truly shared, use std::lock_guard/scoped_lock for mutexes, and pass non-owning views as references or std::span. Validation signals include a clean AddressSanitizer/LeakSanitizer run and Valgrind reporting no leaks or invalid frees. Risk boundary: a leaked or double-freed resource is UB that may only crash in production; if ownership is ambiguous, escalate the design rather than guessing with raw pointers.
